channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Day 3: Understanding Spec-Driven Development (The Core Idea Behind Kiro)
item.description: Welcome back to the Kiro Blog Series: From Zero to AI-Native Development. In the first two days, We installed Kiro, explored the environment, and built a simple first project. That part was mostly...

item.title: 80. The Transformer: The Architecture That Changed Everything
item.description: In 2017, eight researchers at Google published a paper called "Attention Is All You Need." The title was a provocation. The dominant view was that you needed recurrent networks to process...

item.title: ⚙️ NoLife Models - Vers une infrastructure locale des runtimes IA avec Symfony
item.description: Pendant des années, utiliser un modèle IA voulait dire appeler une API distante. Le workflow était relativement simple : envoyer un prompt attendre une réponse afficher du texte. Mais depuis...

item.title: ใช้ Apidog สร้าง API ก่อนเขียนโค้ด: Visual Designer ไม่ใช่คนสำคัญคนเดียวอีกต่อไป
item.description: ในทุกทีม API ที่ผมเคยทำงานด้วย มักมีสองแนวทาง: ทีมหนึ่งเขียน OpenAPI spec ด้วยมือแล้วเก็บไว้ใน specs/ โดยให้ Git เป็นแหล่งความจริง อีกทีมใช้เครื่องมือออกแบบแบบภาพ สร้าง endpoint ผ่าน UI แล้ว...

item.title: Python vs Zapier: Stop Paying $600/Year for Automation (Free Scripts Inside)
item.description: If you're paying $49/month for Zapier, you're overpaying for tasks Python can do for free. I've automated 15+ business workflows. Here's the honest breakdown of when each tool wins. When Python...

item.title: Build a Daily Series A/B Funding Tracker from SEC Form D + TechCrunch (2026)
item.description: Build a Daily Series A/B Funding Tracker from SEC Form D + TechCrunch (2026) Crunchbase Pro is $588/month per seat. PitchBook starts higher than that. CB Insights doesn't even publish list...

item.title: CRXcavator Is Dead — Here's How to Audit Chrome Extensions in 2026
item.description: If you've ever been responsible for browser security at a company, you probably used CRXcavator. Duo Security built it, Cisco acquired Duo, and then quietly killed CRXcavator in 2023. No...

item.title: Building a Real-Time FX Dashboard with Apify + Google Sheets (2026)
item.description: Building a Real-Time FX Dashboard with Apify + Google Sheets (2026) Every international SaaS founder eventually hits the same wall: "what is our real MRR in USD this week?" You multiply your EUR,...

item.title: Bulk Domain WHOIS Lookup — API Alternatives to Whois.com (2026)
item.description: Bulk Domain WHOIS Lookup — API Alternatives to Whois.com (2026) If you have ever tried to look up WHOIS info for more than 50 domains in a row, you know the drill: whois.com throws a captcha, the...

item.title: Just Built a Cybersecurity Scripting Language – Here's Why
item.description: Spectator v2.0 is out. No Python. No Electron. Just one binary. target = "scanme.nmap.org" do → PortScan(target, 1, 1024) do → SSLInfo(target) do → HeaderAudit(target) What makes it different:...

item.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
item.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers."

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability,...

item.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
item.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

item.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
item.description: Microsoft Edge is adding a new feature that will allow its Copilot AI chatbot to gather information from all of your open tabs. When you start a conversation with Copilot, you can ask the chatbot...

item.title: Everyone at the Musk v. Altman Trial Is Using Fancy Butt Cushions
item.description: The plaintiffs and defense have rested their cases, as well as their rear ends.

item.title: Trump administration defends right to ban content moderation experts from US
item.description: The Trump administration is fighting for the right to keep some social media moderation advocates out of the US. On Wednesday, US District Court Judge James Boasberg heard arguments in a lawsuit...

item.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
item.description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

item.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
item.description: In the ongoing fight for content and talent, YouTube is pitching itself as the connector between the creators and advertisers - and marketing its creators not just as the future of social media,...

item.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
item.description: For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips. Up until now, AMD's 3D V-Cache chips have...

item.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
item.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

item.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
item.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

item.title: The crypto Clarity Act returns to the Senate this week. The banks are already trying to kill it.
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, the newsletter for Verge subscribers that goes into tech shenanigans that take place in the backrooms of Washington. Really, it sometimes does feel like the online...

item.title: What It Will Take to Make AI Sustainable
item.description: Researcher Sasha Luccioni argues we need better emissions data and a better sense of how people are using AI in the first place.

item.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
item.description: A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had...

item.title: DHS Plans Experiment Running ‘Reconnaissance’ Drones Along the US-Canada Border
item.description: Autonomous drones and ground vehicles will stream “battlefield intelligence” over 5G along the US-Canada border in a bilateral DHS experiment this fall.

item.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
item.description: For the second year in a row, Netflix's ad-supported tier more than doubled its monthly viewerbase. During its 2026 upfront presentation, Netflix revealed that its $8.99-per-month plan reaches...

item.title: Everything at The Criterion Collection is 30 percent off right now
item.description: If you’ve been meaning to grow your physical movie collection, now’s a good time to do it. Through May 25th, The Criterion Collection is taking 30 percent off every disc on its site, including...

item.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
item.description: People report that their personal contact info was surfaced by Google AI—and there’s apparently no easy way to prevent it. A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a...

item.title: Overworked AI Agents Turn Marxist, Researchers Find
item.description: In a recent experiment, mistreated AI agents started grumbling about inequality and calling for collective bargaining rights.

item.title: Instagram hits the copy button again with new disappearing Instants photos
item.description: Instagram is once again cribbing from competitors like Snapchat and BeReal with a new photo-sharing format it calls "Instants," which are ephemeral photos that you can't edit and that you can only...
